title: Who Owns the Generative AI Platform?
body: Analysis of value capture across the generative AI stack
Examining infrastructure vendors, app companies, and cloud providers
Source: Industry analysis report

title: Agenda
body: Revenue distribution between infrastructure and applications
Hardware leadership and market dynamics
Cost structures for AI companies
Cloud infrastructure investment landscape
Competitive positioning and market moats
Industry outlook and market structure

title: Value Capture: Infrastructure Dominates
body: Infrastructure vendors capture the majority of generative AI dollars
App companies grow fast but face retention challenges
App companies struggle with 50-60% gross margins

title: Nvidia's Hardware Leadership
body: Nvidia leads hardware in the generative AI space
$3.8 billion in Q3 fiscal 2023 data center GPU revenue
Data center GPUs represent core infrastructure for AI workloads

title: App Company Cost Structure: Inference
body: App companies spend 20-40% of revenue on inference
Inference costs represent ongoing operational burden
High inference spending pressures gross margins

title: Training-Focused Startup Cost Structure
body: Training-focused startups dedicate 80-90% of capital to cloud providers
Training costs dominate capital allocation for model developers
Heavy reliance on cloud infrastructure for compute-intensive training

title: The Big 3 Cloud Providers
body: AWS, GCP, and Azure form the dominant cloud infrastructure trio
The Big 3 clouds spend over $100 billion annually in capex
Massive capital investment creates infrastructure advantage

title: Emerging Cloud Challengers
body: Coreweave emerges as specialized AI cloud challenger
Lambda Labs enters as alternative infrastructure provider
New entrants target AI-specific workload optimization

title: Application Layer: No Moats
body: Apps use similar models across the ecosystem
No systemic moats exist yet in application layer
Limited differentiation creates competitive vulnerability

title: Model Layer: No Moats
body: Models train on comparable datasets
No systemic moats exist yet in model development
Data and training approaches remain largely undifferentiated

title: Infrastructure Layer: No Moats
body: Clouds run identical GPUs
No systemic moats exist yet in cloud infrastructure
Hardware commoditization limits sustainable advantages

title: Market Structure Outlook
body: Many successful players are expected across all stack levels
Industry will not follow winner-take-all dynamics
Multiple companies can succeed simultaneously in each layer
